codeReview.summary: The Cedar policy implementation provides core infrastructure for formally verifiable tool access control but has limited integration with the existing tool execution flow. The implementation adds cedar-policy crate, CedarPolicyEngine for authorization, ToolAuthorizationRequest for mapping operations, PolicyValidator for validation, and CedarPolicyBridge for YAML conversion. All 13 unit tests pass.

issues.description: Cedar policy engine is not integrated with the existing tool execution flow. The ToolExecutor in executor.rs uses PermissionChecker with PermissionTier, but CedarPolicyEngine is never used. The cedar_policy module is exported from lib.rs but no code uses it for actual authorization decisions.

Duplicate ToolOperation enum exists alongside opa_policy.rs::ToolOperation. Both define the same operations (Read, Write, Edit, Shell, Git, Search) with risk_level() methods. This duplication could cause maintenance issues and inconsistent behavior.

CedarPolicyEngine::authorize() always uses empty entities via .unwrap_or_else(Entities::empty). Cedar policies typically require entity hierarchy for proper authorization. The entities field is set but never populated with actual data.

ToolAuthorizationRequest::context field is never used - to_cedar_request() always creates Context::empty(). The HashMap<String, cedar_policy::Context> field could misleadingly suggest that context is being passed to Cedar.

PolicyValidator only performs basic text pattern matching for security warnings (checking for '&&' and '||' in source). This is not a substitute for actual Cedar schema validation, which could catch type errors and missing attributes.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The Rust skill documentation mentions tracing for structured logging and thiserror for errors, which were correctly followed. No deviations noted.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: cedar_policy.rs uses tracing::{debug, info, warn} and thiserror::Error correctly.

sharedStateObservations.observation: No additional services or commands were started by the worker beyond the standard cargo build/test workflow. Services.yaml is complete.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows only cargo build --workspace and cargo test commands.

summary: Cedar policy integration is implemented with core components (CedarPolicyEngine, ToolAuthorizationRequest, PolicyValidator, CedarPolicyBridge) and 13 passing unit tests. The implementation correctly uses the cedar-policy crate and follows Rust conventions. However, the Cedar policy system is not integrated into the actual tool execution flow - the existing PermissionChecker in executor.rs continues to handle permissions. There is also duplicate ToolOperation enum between cedar_policy.rs and opa_policy.rs. The implementation fulfills the feature description but integration with the broader system would need follow-up work.
